description_long: This object is a wooden biplane with a classic, vintage design, featuring two parallel wings stacked one above the other and supported by vertical struts. The fuselage is smoothly contoured and crafted from light-colored wood, with a rounded nose and a prominent, dark wooden propeller at the front. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small, white seat visible. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both made from the same wood as the rest of the plane. The landing gear consists of two simple wooden wheels at the front and a small tail skid at the rear. The overall appearance is that of a handcrafted toy or model airplane, with a natural wood finish and visible grain patterns.
description: A handcrafted wooden biplane with stacked wings, a dark wooden propeller, open cockpit, and simple wooden landing gear. The natural wood finish and classic design evoke a vintage toy airplane.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, large dark propeller, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, stacked wings, and the main landing gear with a visible wheel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and the horizontal stabilizer, as well as the open cockpit.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the propeller, wing struts, and the tail skid at the rear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston propeller engine (represented as a wooden propeller).
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
